items.title: PM admits One Nation's rise influenced decision to overhaul tax system

children.content: The rise of One Nation is partly behind the government's decision to take on tax reform in this year's budget, the prime minister has admitted.

articles.title: Multiple One Nation MPs are unclear about their party's housing policy

children.content: After Barnaby Joyce needed a do-over interview to explain One Nation's housing policy, a senator's interview was terminated after it turned into a 'train wreck'.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson waves her hand speaking to the media

articles.title: Hanson takes the stage as budget woes roll on

children.content: While Labor is increasingly concerned about the long-term threat of One Nation, this week its attention was squarely on more immediate problems.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon and white striped tie.

articles.title: Government accused of misrepresenting NDIS cuts in heated hearing

children.content: The federal government has been accused of selling its cuts to the NDIS as a crackdown on fraud, while instead pursuing billions of dollars of dramatic savings by slashing participant packages.

items.title: Sydney police officer sentenced over death of Indigenous 16yo

children.content: A NSW Police officer convicted over a fatal collision that killed an Indigenous teenager has avoided time behind bars.

image.altText: Benedict Bryant in a suit outside court

items.title: Sunscreens that failed to meet SPF claims still under investigation

children.content: Most of the sunscreens that failed to meet their SPF label claims, according to independent testing, are still under investigation by the regulator one year later.

image.altText: Seven sunscreens tested by CHOICE.

items.title: Recorded flu numbers drop drastically but expert warns caution

children.content: Flu cases have come down since the record-breaking 2025 season but experts warn against a "false sense of security".

image.altText: Microscope image of a round red-coloured virus against a blue background.

items.title: Daughter says parents' rural home stabbing 'deeply traumatising'

children.content: The daughter of a couple who suffered critical stab wounds during a suspected home invasion on a remote property said the family is traumatised by the incident.

image.altText: Two women

images.altText: Two women look at a share price board outside the ASX in Sydney.

articles.title: China warns Australia 55pc beef tariffs could apply within days

children.content: China is cracking down on beef imports and says it is ready to slap Australia with an additional 55 per cent tariff in the coming days.

image.altText: Cuts of beef sit displayed in a butcher.

articles.title: What happens when fuel excise cuts end on June 30?

children.content: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

children.content: ASIC has told Senate estimates it has eight active contracts with KPMG, despite the company facing an audit scandal. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: ASIC tells parliament it has eight current contracts with KPMG, despite audit scandal

items.title: Bikie scales dock, escapes Darwin courthouse triggering police manhunt

children.content: A manhunt is under way in Darwin as police search for Brandt Graham, a 44-year-old Mongols bikie who scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody today.

image.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

items.title: Emissions drop again, fuelling hopes Australia's hit a tipping point

children.content: Emissions are at their lowest point since the COVID pandemic shut down the economy, dropping 2.1 per cent over the year to December 2025.

image.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

items.title: This is where Israel's military crossed Lebanon's Litani River

children.content: Images of the first tanks to roll over Lebanon's Litani River were posted online this week. ABC NEWS Verify has found the spot where Israel's military crossed.

articles.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four

children.content: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

items.title: Prolific character actor stabbed to death in front yard at age 81

children.content: James Handy appeared in almost 150 films or TV series, according to his IMDb page, with his last role being a bartender in Top Gun: Maverick.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

items.title: Dorothy the kitten who survived the unthinkable reunited with its rescuer

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

items.title: Need a mood boost? Here's why gardening might be worth a try

children.content: While gardening alone is not a treatment for mental illness, a growing body of evidence links it with higher levels of mental wellbeing and life satisfaction.

items.title: Court system 'in a shambles' after transcription service goes bust

children.content: There are fears mass redundancies at Australia's main transcription service could leave court cases around the country in limbo after it was revealed VIQ Solutions would be wound down.

children.content: Zeinab Ahmad, who recently returned to Australia, is seeking bail after she was charged with slavery offences after allegedly crossing into Syria with her family in 2015.

items.title: Rumours, rifts and a reported resignation form a murky picture of a war-ravaged Iran

children.content: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

items.title: Iranian-French cartoonist Marjane Satrapi, author of Persepolis, dead at 56

children.content: A committed advocate to furthering free speech and women's rights in her birth country, Satrapi lost her husband, Swedish actor Mattias Ripa, in April last year, and "died of sadness", according to French media.

image.altText: A middle-aged woman with dark hair smiles as she stands in front of a film-reel-themed backdrop.

items.title: Sex discrimination commissioner slams gender definition proposal

children.content: Australia's sex discrimination commissioner condemns a proposal to reinstate biological definition of sexes as a "step back" for women.

items.title: Biggest universal magnetic map unlocks new chapter of space research

children.content: Data gathered by scientists at the Square Kilometre Array has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

items.title: The race against time to contain Ebola

children.content: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

items.title: Ancient tribes, military, trade and China's rise meet on this island

children.content: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: What to know about the New World screwworm fly and its reappearance in the US

children.content: The New World screwworm fly is threatening the $US113 billion United States cattle industry for the first time in more than a half century.

items.title: Experts suspect state policy is putting some farmers in artificial drought

children.content: A western New South Wales farmer says dams built next door have stopped all water flows onto his property, forcing him to graze and water his stock by the side of the road.

articles.title: Farmers are increasingly embracing change, and it's bringing them hope

children.content: When grazier Matthew Peart took over his family's organic beef operation almost 20 years ago, his pastures were rundown, and the future looked bleak. But now, he's unashamedly optimistic, and a new report suggests he's not alone.

articles.title: Hot spot for Australian bull semen as China market reopens

children.content: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.
